evals.prompt: I want to add MatrixScan Count to my React Native app. Here's my screen file: EmptyApp.tsx. I need to scan EAN-13 and Code 128 barcodes and show the built-in counting UI.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads integration.md, writes complete BarcodeCount* integration code into EmptyApp.tsx as a function component with hooks, renders a BarcodeCountView with barcodeCount and context props, wires the camera lifecycle, and shows the setup checklist (packages, npx pod-install, camera permission, license key placeholder).

assertions.text: Setup checklist is shown (mentions scandit-react-native-datacapture-core and scandit-react-native-datacapture-barcode)
Setup checklist mentions npx pod-install for iOS
Setup checklist mentions NSCameraUsageDescription in ios Info.plist
DataCaptureContext.initialize( is present (v8 API)
DataCaptureContext.forLicenseKey( is NOT present
DataCaptureContext.sharedInstance is present (semantic)
'-- ENTER YOUR SCANDIT LICENSE KEY HERE --' is present
new BarcodeCount(settings) is present (constructor pattern, not forDataCaptureContext factory)
Symbology.EAN13UPCA is enabled
Symbology.Code128 is enabled
<BarcodeCountView is rendered in the JSX tree
BarcodeCountView has a barcodeCount prop and a context prop (semantic)
view.listener or view.uiListener is set inside the BarcodeCountView ref callback
view.start() is NOT present (BarcodeCountView does not require explicit start)
The camera is switched on with switchToDesiredState (semantic)
The component is written as a function component (no class syntax)
useRef is used to hold the BarcodeCount mode reference
useEffect cleanup calls dataCaptureContext.setFrameSource(null)
Import from 'scandit-react-native-datacapture-core' is present
Import from 'scandit-react-native-datacapture-barcode' is present

evals.prompt: I want to scan items into a list of expected barcodes and visually distinguish the ones that aren't in the list. My expected items are: EAN-13 '4006381333931' (qty 2) and Code 128 'ITEM-001' (qty 1). File: EmptyApp.tsx.
evals.expected_output: The skill integrates BarcodeCount with a BarcodeCountCaptureList containing the specified TargetBarcode entries, sets it on the mode with setBarcodeCountCaptureList, and uses BarcodeCountViewStyle.Dot with notInListBrush so unrecognized items appear differently. The integration includes a BarcodeCountViewListener with didCompleteCaptureList.

assertions.text: BarcodeCountCaptureList.create( is present
TargetBarcode.create( is present with '4006381333931' and quantity 2
TargetBarcode.create( is present with 'ITEM-001' and quantity 1
barcodeCount.setBarcodeCountCaptureList( is present
notInListBrush is assigned on the view (semantic)
<BarcodeCountView is rendered with barcodeCount and context props
BarcodeCountViewStyle.Dot is used as the viewStyle (semantic — required for brushes to take effect)
didCompleteCaptureList is implemented in the view listener (semantic)
'-- ENTER YOUR SCANDIT LICENSE KEY HERE --' is present
DataCaptureContext.initialize( is present
The camera is switched on with switchToDesiredState (semantic)
useEffect cleanup calls dataCaptureContext.setFrameSource(null)

evals.prompt: Hide the exit button and the toolbar from the BarcodeCount view so the screen stays minimal. File: IntegratedApp.tsx.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets shouldShowExitButton = false and shouldShowToolbar = false on the BarcodeCountView in the ref callback. The existing integration code is preserved.

assertions.text: view.shouldShowExitButton = false is present
view.shouldShowToolbar = false is present
Both properties are set inside the BarcodeCountView ref callback (semantic)
Existing BarcodeCount mode, BarcodeCountView JSX, and camera lifecycle are preserved (semantic)
view.listener is still set in the ref callback (semantic)
view.uiListener is still set in the ref callback (semantic)

evals.prompt: Translate all the on-screen hints in my MatrixScan Count screen to German. File: IntegratedApp.tsx.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets all available textFor*Hint properties on the BarcodeCountView in the ref callback using German strings. The existing integration is preserved.

assertions.text: view.textForTapShutterToScanHint is set to a German string
view.textForScanningHint is set to a German string
view.textForMoveCloserAndRescanHint is set to a German string
view.textForMoveFurtherAndRescanHint is set to a German string
view.textForTapToUncountHint is set to a German string (semantic)
All hint properties are set inside the BarcodeCountView ref callback (semantic)
Existing BarcodeCount mode, camera lifecycle, and listeners are preserved (semantic)

evals.prompt: Add a status overlay showing each scanned barcode's stock status after scanning. When status mode is activated, mark barcodes with low stock as LowStock and others as None. File: IntegratedApp.tsx.
evals.expected_output: The skill implements a BarcodeCountStatusProvider with onStatusRequested, wires it via view.setStatusProvider in the ref callback, enables shouldShowStatusModeButton, builds BarcodeCountStatusItem entries, and calls callback.onStatusReady with a BarcodeCountStatusResultSuccess. A note about RN 8.3+ requirement is included.

assertions.text: BarcodeCountStatusProvider is implemented with onStatusRequested
BarcodeCountStatusItem.create( is present
BarcodeCountStatus.LowStock or another BarcodeCountStatus value is used
BarcodeCountStatusResultSuccess.create( is present
callback.onStatusReady( is called (not awaited — callback pattern) (semantic)
view.setStatusProvider( is called inside the BarcodeCountView ref callback
view.shouldShowStatusModeButton = true or view.shouldShowStatusIconsOnScan = true is present
A note or comment about RN 8.3+ requirement is included (semantic)
Existing BarcodeCount mode, camera lifecycle, and view setup are preserved (semantic)

evals.prompt: Let the user uncount a barcode by tapping on its highlight in the BarcodeCount view. File: IntegratedApp.tsx.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets tapToUncountEnabled = true on the BarcodeCountView in the ref callback, and optionally sets textForTapToUncountHint. The existing integration is preserved.

assertions.text: view.tapToUncountEnabled = true is present
tapToUncountEnabled is set inside the BarcodeCountView ref callback (semantic)
textForTapToUncountHint is set or a comment explains the hint text option (semantic)
Existing BarcodeCount mode, camera lifecycle, and listeners are preserved (semantic)

evals.prompt: Customize the recognized-barcode dot highlight to green and the not-in-list highlight to red. Also apply a blue highlight for accepted barcodes and orange for rejected ones. File: IntegratedApp.tsx.
evals.expected_output: The skill switches viewStyle to BarcodeCountViewStyle.Dot (required for brushes to work), sets recognizedBrush to a green Brush, notInListBrush to a red Brush, acceptedBrush to a blue Brush, and rejectedBrush to an orange Brush in the ref callback using Color.fromHex.

assertions.text: BarcodeCountViewStyle.Dot is used as viewStyle prop (semantic — brushes only work in Dot mode)
view.recognizedBrush = new Brush( is present with a green color
view.notInListBrush = new Brush( is present with a red color
view.acceptedBrush = new Brush( is present with a blue color
view.rejectedBrush = new Brush( is present with an orange color
Color.fromHex( is used for color construction
All brush assignments are inside the BarcodeCountView ref callback (semantic)
Existing camera lifecycle and BarcodeCount mode setup are preserved (semantic)

evals.prompt: Keep the counting session alive across the scan and results page transitions. When the user comes back to the scan page, resume counting where they left off. File: IntegratedApp.tsx.
evals.expected_output: The skill preserves the BarcodeCount mode in a useRef across navigations (does not reset on every mount), uses useFocusEffect or similar to restart the camera when the screen re-focuses, and only calls barcodeCount.reset() / clearAdditionalBarcodes() when an explicit reset is needed. The camera is turned off when navigating away.

assertions.text: BarcodeCount mode is held in a useRef so it persists across renders and navigation (semantic)
camera.switchToDesiredState(FrameSourceState.On) is called when the screen is focused or mounted
camera.switchToDesiredState(FrameSourceState.Off) is called when navigating away or exiting (semantic)
barcodeCount.reset() is only called conditionally (not on every mount) (semantic)
useFocusEffect or useEffect with navigation focus guard is used to manage camera state (semantic)
dataCaptureContext.setFrameSource(null) or equivalent cleanup is present

evals.prompt: Enable a hardware trigger key on my Zebra Android device for scanning with BarcodeCount. Use the default hardware key. File: IntegratedApp.tsx.
evals.expected_output: The skill calls view.enableHardwareTrigger(null) inside the BarcodeCountView ref callback after checking BarcodeCountView.hardwareTriggerSupported. A note about the Android API 28+ requirement is included.

assertions.text: view.enableHardwareTrigger(null) is called inside the BarcodeCountView ref callback
BarcodeCountView.hardwareTriggerSupported is checked before enabling the trigger (semantic)
A comment or note about Android API level 28+ requirement is present (semantic)
A comment or note about RN 7.1+ requirement is present (semantic)
Existing BarcodeCount mode, camera lifecycle, and listeners are preserved (semantic)

evals.prompt: My labels carry PDF417 barcodes alongside the ones I want to count. Filter the PDF417 ones out of my MatrixScan Count app so they are not counted. Also exclude any barcode whose data starts with the digits 1234. File: IntegratedApp.tsx.
evals.expected_output: The skill accesses settings.filterSettings (the BarcodeFilterSettings getter on BarcodeCountSettings), sets excludedSymbologies to an array including Symbology.PDF417, and sets excludedCodesRegex to a pattern matching data starting with 1234. The existing integration is preserved.

assertions.text: settings.filterSettings is accessed as the BarcodeFilterSettings getter (semantic)
filterSettings.excludedSymbologies is assigned an array including Symbology.PDF417
filterSettings.excludedCodesRegex is set to a regex matching data starting with 1234 (semantic)
A new BarcodeFilterSettings is NOT constructed and assigned back; the settings.filterSettings getter is mutated in place (semantic)
Existing BarcodeCount mode, camera lifecycle, and listeners are preserved (semantic)

evals.prompt: Every barcode in my batches is unique — no duplicates ever appear. Turn on the optimization for that in my MatrixScan Count app. File: IntegratedApp.tsx.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets settings.expectsOnlyUniqueBarcodes = true on the BarcodeCountSettings before the BarcodeCount mode is constructed. The existing integration is preserved.

assertions.text: settings.expectsOnlyUniqueBarcodes = true is present
expectsOnlyUniqueBarcodes is set on the BarcodeCountSettings before BarcodeCount is constructed (semantic)
Existing BarcodeCount mode, camera lifecycle, and listeners are preserved (semantic)
